title: Sub-pixel Convolutional Layers for 4K Real-Time Video Super-Resolution
abstract: Real-time 4K video super-resolution remains challenging due to the computational cost of conventional upsampling layers. We propose a depth-to-space rearrangement that performs subpixel convolution with 38% fewer FLOPs than nearest-neighbor upsampling followed by 3x3 convolutions, while matching PSNR on the BSD100 and Vimeo90K benchmarks. Our approach achieves 31 FPS on a single RTX 4090 at 4K output resolution. We provide PyTorch and TensorRT implementations.
comment: Accepted to CVPR Workshop on Efficient Vision Models
